senses.definition: not affected by alcohol; not drunk
examples: She remained sober after the long night of celebrations.
The doctor advised him to stay sober for 24 hours before the test.

senses.definition: plain or serious in style; not decorative or showy
examples: They wore sober clothes for the funeral.
The design is sober and professional.

senses.definition: a person who is not intoxicated; someone who behaves in a serious or sensible way
examples: He tuned his thoughts to sobriety, becoming a sober citizen.
In many cultures, the sober are given respect.

senses.definition: to make sober or more serious; to bring into a more serious mood
examples: The news sobered him up quickly.
A wake-up call sobers the mind.

senses.definition: to become or make someone more sober or serious, often after drunkenness or excitement
examples: The announcement sobered him suddenly.
A harsh reminder sobered the crowd.

senses.definition: comparative form of sober: more serious or less intoxicated
examples: He looked a bit soberer after the conversation.
A soberer mood settled over the room.

senses.definition: comparative form indicating the most sober or most serious
examples: Of all the guests, she was the soberest.
The soberest reading of the data suggested a pause.

senses.definition: causing a sober or serious mood; making you think seriously about something
examples: The accident was a sobering reminder of road safety.
The report gave a sobering assessment of the economy.

senses.definition: in a way that makes you feel serious or thoughtful
examples: The statistics spoke soberingly about the challenges ahead.
Soberingly, the costs are rising faster than inflation.

senses.definition: to make someone sober or more sober; to make serious
examples: A night out can soberise you after a long day.
It can soberise the debate.

senses.definition: past tense of soberise; made sober or serious
examples: He soberised after the meeting.
The news soberised the crowd.

senses.definition: third-person singular of soberise; makes sober or serious
examples: It soberises the mood in critical thinking.
The coach soberises the team before the game.

senses.definition: present participle of soberise; making someone sober or serious
examples: The announcement is soberising the audience.
They are soberising their stance on the issue.

senses.definition: to make sober or more serious
examples: We need to soberize our budgeting approach.
The warnings will soberize the crowd.

senses.definition: past tense of soberize; made sober or more serious
examples: The warning soberized the crowd.
Policy changes soberized the debate.

senses.definition: third-person singular of soberize; makes sober or more serious
examples: It soberizes the discussion.
Policy changes soberizes the public mood.

senses.definition: present participle of soberize; making someone sober or more serious
examples: Soberizing the audience took some time.
They are soberizing their approach to the budget.

senses.definition: resembling or similar to being sober; serious in appearance or manner
examples: Her soberlike attitude impressed the committee.
A soberlike tone pervaded the report.

senses.definition: in a sober manner; without excess or emotion; calmly and seriously
examples: She spoke soberly about the risks.
The report was written soberly and clearly.

senses.definition: the state of being sober or free from alcohol; seriousness
examples: He embraced soberness after the scandal.
Soberness is valued in professional settings.

senses.definition: plural of soberness; multiple instances or forms of seriousness or temperance
examples: Their sobernesses varied across departments.
Different cultures show sobernesses in different ways.

senses.definition: plural of sober; not drunk people collectively (informal/rare)
examples: The party grew quiet as the sobers returned home.
Two sobers remained after the celebrations.

senses.definition: a misspelling or playful form of somersault; a flip or tumble
examples: He performed an energetic sobersault in the gym.
The cartoon showed a dramatic sobersault.

senses.definition: having sober sides; dressed or engineered with a sober, calm appearance
examples: A sobersided table stood in the hall.
The car has sobersided panels for a discreet look.

senses.definition: in a sobersided manner; with a calm or sober look
examples: She nodded sobersidedly and continued.
He replied sobersidedly, without emotion.

senses.definition: the state or quality of being sobersided; calmness or seriousness
examples: The sobersidedness of the room was felt by all.
A mood of sobersidedness accompanied the ceremony.

senses.definition: plural of sobersidedness
examples: Various sobersidednesses were observed across committees.

senses.definition: plural of sober side; the calm, serious aspects of something
examples: We considered the sobersides of the project.
The sobersides of the argument were overlooked.
